rebound-tutorial-0.1.0.0
  • Contents
  • Index
Safe HaskellSafe-Inferred
LanguageGHC2021

Tutorial.Scoped.Syntax

Description

A simply-typed lambda calculus with binary products and sums whose terms are indexed by a Nat tracking the number of free variables in scope. Variable occurrences are represented as 'Fin n' indices (de Bruijn style), and the rebound library's Bind1 / Bind2 abstractions handle binding with pretty-printing hints stored in LocalName values.

Synopsis
  • data Ty
    • = One
    • | Zero
    • | Ty :-> Ty
    • | Ty :* Ty
    • | Ty :+ Ty
  • data Tm (n :: Nat) where
    • Var :: Fin n -> Tm n
    • Lam :: Bind1 Tm Tm n -> Tm n
    • Unit :: Tm n
    • Pair :: Tm n -> Tm n -> Tm n
    • Inj :: Int -> Tm n -> Tm n
    • App :: Tm n -> Tm n -> Tm n
    • MatchUnit :: Tm n -> Tm n -> Tm n
    • MatchPair :: Tm n -> Bind2 Tm Tm n -> Tm n
    • MatchSum :: Tm n -> Bind1 Tm Tm n -> Bind1 Tm Tm n -> Tm n

  • class Sized t where
    • type Size t :: Nat
    • size :: t -> SNat (Size t)

  • data Env (a :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat)
  • class SNatI (n :: Nat) where
    • induction :: f 'Z -> (forall (m :: Nat). SNatI m => f m -> f ('S m)) -> f n
  • data SNat (n :: Nat) where
    • SZ :: SNat 'Z
    • SS :: forall (n1 :: Nat). SNatI n1 => SNat ('S n1)
    • pattern SS' :: forall m n. () => m ~ 'S n => SNat n -> SNat m
  • data Fin (n :: Nat) where
    • FZ :: forall (n1 :: Nat). Fin ('S n1)
    • FS :: forall (n1 :: Nat). Fin n1 -> Fin ('S n1)
  • newtype Refinement (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) = Refinement (Map (Fin n) (v n))

    • VNil :: forall a. Vec 'Z a
    • (:::) :: forall a (n1 :: Nat). a -> Vec n1 a -> Vec ('S n1) a

    • name :: String
    }

    • SZ_ :: SNat_ 'Z
    • SS_ :: forall (n1 :: Nat). SNat n1 -> SNat_ ('S n1)

    • toInt :: a -> Int

  • type N1 = 'S N0
  • type N0 = 'Z

    • gsubst :: forall (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). Env v m n -> e m -> e n

    • applyE :: forall (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). Env v n m -> c n -> c m
    • isVar :: forall (n :: Nat). c n -> Maybe (v :~: c, Fin n)
  • class Subst v v => SubstVar (v :: Nat -> Type) where
    • var :: forall (n :: Nat). Fin n -> v n

  • class PatEq t1 t2 where
    • patEq :: t1 -> t2 -> Maybe (Size t1 :~: Size t2)
  • class GStrengthen (t :: Nat -> Type) where
    • gstrengthenRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat m -> SNat n -> t (k + (m + n)) -> Maybe (t (k + n))
  • class Strengthen (t :: Nat -> Type) where
    • strengthenRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat m -> SNat n -> t (k + (m + n)) -> Maybe (t (k + n))
    • strengthenOneRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat n -> t (k + 'S n) -> Maybe (t (k + n))

    • gappearsFree :: forall (n :: Nat). Fin n -> t n -> Bool
    • gfreeVars :: forall (n :: Nat). t n -> Set (Fin n)
  • class FV (t :: Nat -> Type) where
    • appearsFree :: forall (n :: Nat). Fin n -> t n -> Bool
    • freeVars :: forall (n :: Nat). t n -> Set (Fin n)

    • shift :: forall (k :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> t n -> t (k + n)

data Tm (n :: Nat) where #

Terms of the simply-typed lambda calculus, parameterised by the number of free variables n in scope. Variable occurrences are de Bruijn indices ('Fin n'); binders use Bind1 (one new variable) or Bind2 (two new variables).

Constructors

Var :: Fin n -> Tm n

Variable occurrence — a de Bruijn index into scope n

Lam :: Bind1 Tm Tm n -> Tm n

Lambda abstraction — binds one variable

Unit :: Tm n

Unit value ()

Pair :: Tm n -> Tm n -> Tm n

Binary pair (e1, e2)

Inj :: Int -> Tm n -> Tm n

Injection into a sum: Inj 0 e or Inj 1 e

App :: Tm n -> Tm n -> Tm n

Function application

MatchUnit :: Tm n -> Tm n -> Tm n

Unit elimination — match scrutinee against (), then evaluate body

MatchPair :: Tm n -> Bind2 Tm Tm n -> Tm n

Pair elimination — bind both components in body

MatchSum :: Tm n -> Bind1 Tm Tm n -> Bind1 Tm Tm n -> Tm n

Sum elimination — one branch for each injection, each binding its payload

Subst Tm Cont #

Enables applyE to apply substitution environments to Cont values, which is needed when weakening continuations into larger scopes.

S1 ('MetaSel ('Nothing :: Maybe Symbol) 'NoSourceUnpackedness 'NoSourceStrictness 'DecidedLazy) (Rec1 (Bind Tm Tm LocalName))))))))

class a ~# b => (a :: k0) ~~ (b :: k1) infix 4 #

Lifted, heterogeneous equality. By lifted, we mean that it can be bogus (deferred type error). By heterogeneous, the two types a and b might have different kinds. Because ~~ can appear unexpectedly in error messages to users who do not care about the difference between heterogeneous equality ~~ and homogeneous equality ~, this is printed as ~ unless -fprint-equality-relations is set.

In 0.7.0, the fixity was set to infix 4 to match the fixity of :~~:.

class a ~# b => (a :: k) ~ (b :: k) infix 4 #

Lifted, homogeneous equality. By lifted, we mean that it can be bogus (deferred type error). By homogeneous, the two types a and b must have the same kinds.

type Type = TYPE LiftedRep #

The kind of types with lifted values. For example Int :: Type.

class Generic a where #

Representable types of kind *. This class is derivable in GHC with the DeriveGeneric flag on.

A Generic instance must satisfy the following laws:

from . to ≡ id
to . from ≡ id

Associated Types

type Rep a :: Type -> Type #

Generic representation type

Methods

from :: a -> Rep a x #

Convert from the datatype to its representation

to :: Rep a x -> a #

Convert from the representation to the datatype

type family (a :: k) == (b :: k) :: Bool where ... infix 4 #

A type family to compute Boolean equality.

Equations

(f a :: k2) == (g b :: k2) = (f == g) && (a == b) 
(a :: k) == (a :: k) = 'True 
(_1 :: k) == (_2 :: k) = 'False 

class Generic1 (f :: k -> Type) where #

Representable types of kind * -> * (or kind k -> *, when PolyKinds is enabled). This class is derivable in GHC with the DeriveGeneric flag on.

A Generic1 instance must satisfy the following laws:

from1 . to1 ≡ id
to1 . from1 ≡ id

Associated Types

type Rep1 (f :: k -> Type) :: k -> Type #

Generic representation type

Methods

from1 :: forall (a :: k). f a -> Rep1 f a #

Convert from the datatype to its representation

to1 :: forall (a :: k). Rep1 f a -> f a #

Convert from the representation to the datatype

Instances

Instances details

Generic1 (List a :: Nat -> Type)

Enable generic programming for the List type We can't derive the Generic1 instance for List using newtype deriving because the kinds differ. Therefore we need to write it by hand.

Instance details

Defined in Data.Scoped.List

Associated Types

type Rep1 (List a) :: k -> Type #

Methods

from1 :: forall (a0 :: k). List a a0 -> Rep1 (List a) a0 #

type family (n :: Nat) + (m :: Nat) :: Nat where ... #

Notation for the addition of naturals.

Equations

m + n = Plus m n 

class TestEquality (f :: k -> Type) where #

This class contains types where you can learn the equality of two types from information contained in terms.

The result should be Just Refl if and only if the types applied to f are equal:

TestEquality (x :: f a) (y :: f b) = Just Refl ⟺ a = b

Typically, only singleton types should inhabit this class. In that case type argument equality coincides with term equality:

TestEquality (x :: f a) (y :: f b) = Just Refl ⟺ a = b ⟺ x = y
isJust (TestEquality x y) = x == y

Singleton types are not required, however, and so the latter two would-be laws are not in fact valid in general.

Methods

testEquality :: forall (a :: k) (b :: k). f a -> f b -> Maybe (a :~: b) #

Conditionally prove the equality of a and b.

Instances

Instances details
TestEquality SNat 
Instance details

Defined in Data.Type.Nat

Methods

testEquality :: forall (a :: k) (b :: k). SNat a -> SNat b -> Maybe (a :~: b) #

testEquality :: forall (a0 :: k0) (b :: k0). (a :~~: a0) -> (a :~~: b) -> Maybe (a0 :~: b) #

TestEquality f => TestEquality (Compose f g :: k2 -> Type)

The deduction (via generativity) that if g x :~: g y then x :~: y.

Since: base-4.14.0.0

Instance details

Defined in Data.Functor.Compose

Methods

testEquality :: forall (a :: k) (b :: k). Compose f g a -> Compose f g b -> Maybe (a :~: b) #

data (a :: k1) :~~: (b :: k2) where infix 4 #

Kind heterogeneous propositional equality. Like :~:, a :~~: b is inhabited by a terminating value if and only if a is the same type as b.

Since: base-4.10.0.0

data (a :: k) :~: (b :: k) where infix 4 #

Propositional equality. If a :~: b is inhabited by some terminating value, then the type a is the same as the type b. To use this equality in practice, pattern-match on the a :~: b to get out the Refl constructor; in the body of the pattern-match, the compiler knows that a ~ b.

Since: base-4.7.0.0

Refl :: forall {k} (a :: k). a :~: a

EqP Fin
>>> eqp FZ FZ
True

>>> eqp FZ (FS FZ)
False

>>> let xs = universe @N.Nat4; ys = universe @N.Nat6 in traverse_ print [ [ eqp x y | y <- ys ] | x <- xs ]
[True,False,False,False,False,False]
[False,True,False,False,False,False]
[False,False,True,False,False,False]
[False,False,False,True,False,False]

Since: fin-0.2.2

Generic1 (List a :: Nat -> Type)

Enable generic programming for the List type We can't derive the Generic1 instance for List using newtype deriving because the kinds differ. Therefore we need to write it by hand.

type Rep1 (List a :: Nat -> Type) 
Instance details

Defined in Data.Scoped.List

type Rep1 (List a :: Nat -> Type) = (U1 :: Nat -> Type) :+: (Rec1 a :*: Rec1 (List a))

class Sized t where #

Calculate the number of binding variables in the pattern This number does not need to be an explicit parameter of the type so that we have flexibility about what types we can use as patterns.

data Env (a :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat) #

class SNatI (n :: Nat) where #

Implicit SNat.

In an unorthodox singleton way, it actually provides an induction function.

The induction should often be fully inlined. See test/Inspection.hs.

>>> :set -XPolyKinds
>>> newtype Const a b = Const a deriving (Show)
>>> induction (Const 0) (coerce ((+2) :: Int -> Int)) :: Const Int Nat3
Const 6

Methods

induction #

Arguments

:: f 'Z

zero case

-> (forall (m :: Nat). SNatI m => f m -> f ('S m))

induction step

-> f n

data SNat (n :: Nat) where #

Singleton of Nat.

Constructors

SZ :: SNat 'Z 
SS :: forall (n1 :: Nat). SNatI n1 => SNat ('S n1) 

Bundled Patterns

pattern SS' :: forall m n. () => m ~ 'S n => SNat n -> SNat m

A pattern with explicit argument

>>> let predSNat :: SNat (S n) -> SNat n; predSNat (SS' n) = n
>>> predSNat (SS' (SS' SZ))
SS

>>> reflect $ predSNat (SS' (SS' SZ))
1

Since: fin-0.3.2

data Fin (n :: Nat) where #

Finite numbers: [0..n-1].

Instance details

Defined in Rebound.Classes

Methods

Strengthen Fin 
Instance details

Defined in Rebound.Classes

Methods

strengthenRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat m -> SNat n -> Fin (k + (m + n)) -> Maybe (Fin (k + n)) #

strengthenOneRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat n -> Fin (k + 'S n) -> Maybe (Fin (k + n)) #

EqP Fin
>>> eqp FZ FZ
True

>>> eqp FZ (FS FZ)
False

>>> let xs = universe @N.Nat4; ys = universe @N.Nat6 in traverse_ print [ [ eqp x y | y <- ys ] | x <- xs ]
[True,False,False,False,False,False]
[False,True,False,False,False,False]
[False,False,True,False,False,False]
[False,False,False,True,False,False]

Since: fin-0.2.2

Instance details

Defined in Data.Fin

Methods

OrdP Fin
>>> let xs = universe @N.Nat4; ys = universe @N.Nat6 in traverse_ print [ [ comparep x y | y <- ys ] | x <- xs ]
[EQ,LT,LT,LT,LT,LT]
[GT,EQ,LT,LT,LT,LT]
[GT,GT,EQ,LT,LT,LT]
[GT,GT,GT,EQ,LT,LT]

Since: fin-0.2.2

Methods

succ :: Fin n -> Fin n #

pred :: Fin n -> Fin n #

toEnum :: Int -> Fin n #

fromEnum :: Fin n -> Int #

enumFrom :: Fin n -> [Fin n] #

SNatI n => Num (Fin n)

Operations module n.

>>> map fromInteger [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, -5] :: [Fin N.Nat3]
[0,1,2,0,1,1]

>>> fromInteger 42 :: Fin N.Nat0
*** Exception: divide by zero
...

>>> signum (FZ :: Fin N.Nat1)
0

>>> signum (3 :: Fin N.Nat4)
1

>>> 2 + 3 :: Fin N.Nat4
1

>>> 2 * 3 :: Fin N.Nat4
2

newtype Refinement (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) #

A refinement is a special kind of substitution that does not change the scope, it just replaces all uses of a particular variable with some other term, which lives in the same scope.

Constructors

Refinement (Map (Fin n) (v n)) 

Instances

Instances details
Shiftable v => Shiftable (Refinement v) 
Instance details

Defined in Rebound.Refinement

Methods

newtype LocalName #

A simple wrapper for strings All local names are *equal* so that when they are used as patterns they will be ignored.

Apply the environment throughout a term of type `c n`, replacing variables with values of type `v m`

class GStrengthen (t :: Nat -> Type) where #

Generic programming support for Strengthen.

Methods

gstrengthenRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat m -> SNat n -> t (k + (m + n)) -> Maybe (t (k + n)) #

class Strengthen (t :: Nat -> Type) where #

Bring scoped terms into a smaller scope, if possible.

Strengthening is only possible if the term only refers to variable which are in the smaller scope.

Minimal complete definition

Nothing

Methods

strengthenRec :: forall (k :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> SNat m -> SNat n -> t (k + (m + n)) -> Maybe (t (k + n)) #

class Shiftable (t :: Nat -> Type) where #

Bring a scoped type into a new, bigger, scope, through variable shifting.

This class is used for types which are scoped, yet do not allow substitution in general. Typical examples are data-structures which associate metadata to variables. See Refinement for an example.

Methods

shift :: forall (k :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> t n -> t (k + n) #

Instances

Instances details

Instance details

Defined in Rebound.Refinement

The substitution operation composes the explicit substitution with the one stored at the binder

Methods

shift :: forall (k :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SNat k -> Bind v c p n -> Bind v c p (k + n) #

apply :: forall {k1} {k2} (f :: k1 -> k2) (g :: k1 -> k2) (a :: k1) (b :: k1). (f :~: g) -> (a :~: b) -> f a :~: g b #

Apply one equality to another, respectively

tabulate :: forall (n :: Nat) v (m :: Nat). (SNatI n, Subst v v) => Env v n m -> [(Fin n, v m)] #

Convert an environment to an association list.

head :: forall v (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). SubstVar v => Env v ('S n) m -> v m #

Access the term at index 0.

tail :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). SubstVar v => Env v ('S n) m -> Env v n m #

uncons an environment, removing the mapping for index '0'. All other keys are shifted back.

(+++) :: forall v (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => Ctx v n -> v n -> Ctx v ('S n) #

Snoc a new definition to the end of the context All existing types in the context need to be shifted (lazily)

sym :: forall {k} (a :: k) (b :: k). (a :~: b) -> b :~: a #

Symmetry of equality

trans :: forall {k} (a :: k) (b :: k) (c :: k). (a :~: b) -> (b :~: c) -> a :~: c #

Transitivity of equality

castWith :: (a :~: b) -> a -> b #

Type-safe cast, using propositional equality

gcastWith :: forall {k} (a :: k) (b :: k) r. (a :~: b) -> (a ~ b => r) -> r #

Generalized form of type-safe cast using propositional equality

inner :: forall {k1} {k2} (f :: k1 -> k2) (a :: k1) (g :: k1 -> k2) (b :: k1). (f a :~: g b) -> a :~: b #

Extract equality of the arguments from an equality of applied types

outer :: forall {k1} {k2} (f :: k1 -> k2) (a :: k1) (g :: k1 -> k2) (b :: k1). (f a :~: g b) -> f :~: g #

Extract equality of type constructors from an equality of applied types

skip :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => Env v m n -> Env v m ('S n) #

rename then increment by 1

explicitShow :: Nat -> String #

show displaying a structure of Nat.

>>> explicitShow 0
"Z"

>>> explicitShow 2
"S (S Z)"

explicitShowsPrec :: Int -> Nat -> ShowS #

showsPrec displaying a structure of Nat.

cata :: a -> (a -> a) -> Nat -> a #

Fold Nat.

>>> cata [] ('x' :) 2
"xx"

toNatural :: Nat -> Natural #

Convert Nat to Natural

>>> toNatural 0
0

>>> toNatural 2
2

>>> toNatural $ S $ S $ Z
2

fromNatural :: Natural -> Nat #

Convert Natural to Nat

>>> fromNatural 4
4

>>> explicitShow (fromNatural 4)
"S (S (S (S Z)))"

nat0 :: Nat #

nat1 :: Nat #

nat2 :: Nat #

nat3 :: Nat #

nat4 :: Nat #

nat5 :: Nat #

nat6 :: Nat #

nat7 :: Nat #

nat8 :: Nat #

nat9 :: Nat #

snat :: forall (n :: Nat). SNatI n => SNat n #

Construct explicit SNat value.

withSNat :: forall (n :: Nat) r. SNat n -> (SNatI n => r) -> r #

Constructor SNatI dictionary from SNat.

Since: fin-0.0.3

reflect :: forall (n :: Nat) proxy. SNatI n => proxy n -> Nat #

Reflect type-level Nat to the term level.

reify :: Nat -> (forall (n :: Nat). SNatI n => Proxy n -> r) -> r #

Reify Nat.

>>> reify nat3 reflect
3

snatToNat :: forall (n :: Nat). SNat n -> Nat #

Convert SNat to Nat.

>>> snatToNat (snat :: SNat Nat1)
1

rawEq :: LocalName -> LocalName -> Bool #

internalName :: LocalName #

A default name

wildcardName :: LocalName #

axiomPlusZ :: forall (m :: Nat). (m + 'Z) :~: m #

'0' is identity element for +

axiomAssoc :: forall (p :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). (p + (m + n)) :~: ((p + m) + n) #

+ is associative.

s0 :: SNat N0 #

s1 :: SNat N1 #

s2 :: SNat N2 #

s3 :: SNat N3 #

sPlus :: forall (n1 :: Nat) (n2 :: Nat). SNat n1 -> SNat n2 -> SNat (n1 + n2) #

Addition of singleton naturals.

snat_ :: forall (n :: Nat). SNat n -> SNat_ n #

View pattern allowing pattern matching on naturals.

f :: forall p. SNat p -> ...
f SZ = ...
f (snat_ -> SS_ m) = ...

prev :: forall (n :: Nat). SNat ('S n) -> SNat n #

Predecessor of a natural.

next :: forall (n :: Nat). SNat n -> SNat ('S n) #

Successor of a natural.

gapplyE :: forall c (v :: Nat -> Type) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). (Generic1 c, GSubst v (Rep1 c), Subst v c) => Env v m n -> c m -> c n #

Generic programming variant of applyE.

applyEnv :: forall a (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). SubstVar a => Env a n m -> Fin n -> a m #

Value of the index x in the substitution s

applyOpt :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat) c. (Env v n m -> c n -> c m) -> Env v n m -> c n -> c m #

Build an optimized version of applyE. Checks to see if we are applying the identity substitution first.

zeroE :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). Env v 'Z n #

The empty environment (zero domain)

weakenER :: forall (m :: Nat) (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => SNat m -> Env v n (n + m) #

Increase the bound on free variables (on the right), without changing any free variable.

weakenE' :: forall (m :: Nat) (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => SNat m -> Env v n (m + n) #

Increase the bound on free variables (on the left), without changing any free variable.

shiftNE :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => SNat m -> Env v n (m + n) #

Shift the term, increasing every free variable as well as the bound by the provided amount.

(.:) :: forall v (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). v m -> Env v n m -> Env v ('S n) m #

cons an environment, adding a new mapping for index '0'. All keys are shifted over.

(.>>) :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (p :: Nat) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). Subst v v => Env v p n -> Env v n m -> Env v p m #

Compose two environments, applying them in sequence (left then right). Some optimizations will be applied to optimize the resulting environment.

up :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => Env v m n -> Env v ('S m) ('S n) #

Adapt an environment to go under a binder.

transform :: forall b a (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). SubstVar b => (forall (m1 :: Nat). a m1 -> b m1) -> Env a n m -> Env b n m #

Map the range of an environment. Has to preserve the scope of the range.

strengthen :: forall (n :: Nat) t. (Strengthen t, SNatI n) => t ('S n) -> Maybe (t n) #

Eliminates the most recently bound variable from the term (if unused).

strengthenN :: forall (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat) t. (Strengthen t, SNatI n) => SNat m -> t (m + n) -> Maybe (t n) #

Eliminates the n most recently bound variables from the term (if unused).

rescope :: forall (n :: Nat) (k :: Nat). SNat k -> Set (Fin (k + n)) -> Set (Fin n) #

Update a set of free variables to a new scope through strengthening

oneE :: forall v (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => v n -> Env v ('S 'Z) n #

A singleton environment (single index domain), which maps that single variable to the provided term.

singletonE :: forall v (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => v n -> Env v ('S n) n #

An environment that maps index 0 to the provided term, and maps all other indices to themselves.

idE :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => Env v n n #

An identity environment, which maps all indices to themselves.

(.++) :: forall (p :: Nat) (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). (SNatI p, SubstVar v) => Env v p n -> Env v m n -> Env v (p + m) n #

append two environments The SNatI constraint is a runtime witness for the length of the domain of the first environment.

appendE :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (p :: Nat) (n :: Nat) (m :: Nat). SubstVar v => SNat p -> Env v p n -> Env v m n -> Env v (p + m) n #

Append two environments, with the length SNat p explicitly required.

If the length is implicitly available, .++ might be preferable.

shift1E :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => Env v n ('S n) #

Increment all free variables in image by 1.

upN :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (p :: Nat) (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => SNat p -> Env v m n -> Env v (p + m) (p + n) #

Shift an environment by size p

shiftFromApplyE :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) c (k :: Nat) (n :: Nat). (SubstVar v, Subst v c) => SNat k -> c n -> c (k + n) #

Allow to implement Shiftable using Subst.

fromVec :: forall v (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => Vec m (v n) -> Env v m n #

Convert an environment to a Vec.

toVec :: forall v (m :: Nat) (n :: Nat). SubstVar v => SNat m -> Env v m n -> Vec m (v n) #

Convert a Vec to an environment.

fromTable :: forall (n :: Nat) v. (SNatI n, SubstVar v) => [(Fin n, v n)] -> Env v n n #

Convert an association list to an environment.

emptyC :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type). Ctx v N0 #

An empty context, that includes no variable assumptions

(++++) :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat) (n' :: Nat) (m :: Nat). (SNatI n', SubstVar v) => Env v n m -> Env v n' (n' + m) -> Env v (n' + n) (n' + m) #

Append contexts. Shifts all indices in the first argument by the length of the second.

emptyR :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). Refinement v n #

The empty refinement. Maps every variable to itself.

joinR :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). (SNatI n, Subst v v, Eq (v n)) => Refinement v n -> Refinement v n -> Maybe (Refinement v n) #

Joinmergemeld two refinements. Fails if the two refinements have overlapping domains.

singletonR :: forall v (n :: Nat). (SubstVar v, Eq (v n)) => (Fin n, v n) -> Refinement v n #

A singleton refinement. Maps the specified variable to the specified term, and every other variable gets mapped to itself.

toEnvironment :: forall (n :: Nat) (v :: Nat -> Type). (SNatI n, SubstVar v) => Refinement v n -> Env v n n #

Convert a refinement into an environment.

fromEnvironment :: forall (n :: Nat) (v :: Nat -> Type). (SNatI n, SubstVar v) => Env v n n -> Refinement v n #

Convert a refinement to an environment.

refine :: forall (n :: Nat) (v :: Nat -> Type) c. (SNatI n, Subst v c) => Refinement v n -> c n -> c n #

Apply the refinement to a variable.

domain :: forall (v :: Nat -> Type) (n :: Nat). Refinement v n -> [Fin n] #

Returns the domain of the environment (i.e., the list of refined variables).
